description: Free tuition at state institutions for dependents of 100% P&T veterans.
how_to_claim: Apply through Alabama Department of Veterans Affairs

description: VA disability compensation is exempt from Alabama state income tax.
how_to_claim: No action needed - VA compensation is not taxable

description: Alabama Disabled Veteran Homestead Exemption — full exemption from all state, county, AND city ad valorem taxes on the homestead (single-family owner-occupied home + up to 160 acres of land). Eligibility: veteran (any age) with VA-determined service-connected PERMANENT AND TOTAL disability rating, OR a veteran whose home was acquired with a VA Specially Adapted Housing Grant (this category is exempt regardless of disability rating). NO income limit applies for P&T veterans. Surviving spouse continues the exemption while occupying the home. Apply at the county tax assessor with VA disability documentation.
value_description: Full property tax exemption — typical savings $800–$3,500/yr depending on county
how_to_claim: Apply at county tax assessor with VA P&T award letter or evidence of Specially Adapted Housing Grant
agency: County Tax Assessor / AL Dept of Revenue

description: Alabama Disabled Military Veteran's Appreciation Licenses — TIERED, NOT fully free. (1) 100% Disabled Veteran Appreciation Hunting License: $2 fee plus issuance fee. (2) 40%+ disabled veterans can purchase a LIFETIME disabled veteran appreciation hunting license at heavily reduced cost. (3) 20%+ disabled veterans qualify for the Annual Military Veteran's Appreciation freshwater/saltwater fishing license at reduced rate. All tiers require Alabama residency and a VA letter certifying disability percentage. Apply at local probate office or by mail to AL DCNR.
value_description: $2 hunting license for 100%; tiered reduced-rate licenses for 20–99% — typical value $50–$200/yr
how_to_claim: Apply at local probate office or by mail to AL DCNR with VA disability letter
agency: AL Dept of Conservation and Natural Resources

description: Free distinctive license plate for disabled veterans. 100% disabled get free registration.
how_to_claim: Apply at county licensing office with DD-214 and VA letter

description: In-state tuition waiver at state universities and community colleges for disabled veterans.
how_to_claim: Contact school veterans office

description: Arizona 100% Service-Connected Veterans' Personal Exemption — for 2026, veterans with a 100% service-connected disability rating receive a FULL exemption from property tax on their primary residence with NO cap on property value (per Prop 130 / SB1749 reform). Income test still applies: must meet the same 2026 income criteria as the partial exemption ($39,865 household, $47,826 with minor children). NOT automatic — requires application approval by County Assessor. Surviving spouse may continue while occupying home. Apply at County Assessor.
value_description: Full primary-residence property tax exemption when income test met — typical savings $1,500–$5,000/yr
how_to_claim: Apply at County Assessor with VA Summary of Benefits Letter showing 100% rating and income documentation

description: Arizona Disabled Veterans' Property Tax Exemption (partial) — for 2026, $4,873 of assessed value is exempt, MULTIPLIED by the veteran's VA disability percentage (e.g., 60% rating × $4,873 = $2,924 of assessed value exempt). Income test applies: 2026 household income must not exceed $39,865 (or $47,826 with minor children in the home). Must be a legal Arizona resident. Submit a Summary of Benefits Letter from the VA. Apply at the County Assessor between the first Monday in January and March 1.
value_description: $4,873 × disability % of assessed value exempt — typical savings $30–$400/yr
how_to_claim: Apply at County Assessor between first Monday in January and March 1 with VA Summary of Benefits Letter and income documentation

description: Free entry to all Arizona state parks for disabled veterans.
how_to_claim: Show VA ID at park entrance

description: Free license plate for disabled veterans.
how_to_claim: Apply at MVD office

description: CalVet fee waiver at CA community colleges, CSU, and UC for eligible disabled veterans and dependents.
how_to_claim: Apply through school financial aid office

benefit_name: No State Income Tax on VA Pay
description: VA disability compensation is exempt from CA state income tax.
how_to_claim: No action needed

description: California Disabled Veterans' Exemption — for 2026, exempts up to $180,671 (Basic) or $271,009 (Low-Income) of the home's assessed value from property tax on the primary residence. Eligibility: veteran must be rated 100% service-connected disabled OR compensated at the 100% rate due to TDIU/individual unemployability. Low-Income tier requires household income at or below $81,131 in 2026 (income includes VA disability compensation). NOT a full exemption — value above the exempt amount remains taxable at the local rate (typically 1.0–1.25%). Unremarried surviving spouse may also qualify. Apply at County Assessor.
value_description: Up to $271,009 of assessed value exempt — typical savings $1,800–$3,000/yr
how_to_claim: Apply at County Assessor with VA letter showing 100% rating or TDIU; income documentation for Low-Income tier

description: Free day-use entry to all California state parks for 50%+ disabled veterans.
how_to_claim: Obtain DPR pass at any state park
agency: CA Dept of Parks and Recreation

description: California Disabled Veteran License Plates and Fee Waiver — DV plates issued free of charge, AND vehicle registration + license fees fully WAIVED for one passenger vehicle, motorcycle, or commercial vehicle with unladen weight ≤8,000 lbs. AB-390 (effective 2026): replacement DV plates also free. Eligibility: veteran must have a SINGLE condition rated 100% by VA or military service (combined ratings totaling 100% do NOT qualify) — must be a diagnosed disease/disorder that substantially impairs mobility. Surviving unremarried spouse may continue benefit.
value_description: Plate + full registration fee waived ($200–$800/yr depending on vehicle)
how_to_claim: Apply at CA DMV with VA letter showing single 100% condition rating

description: Free tuition at state schools for dependents of 100% P&T veterans or those killed/MIA.
how_to_claim: Apply through CO Dept of Higher Education

description: Colorado Disabled Veteran / Gold Star Spouse Property Tax Exemption — 50% exemption on the FIRST $200,000 of actual home value on the primary residence (so up to $100,000 of value shielded). Eligibility (any of): (1) 100% permanent and total service-connected rating from VA; (2) TDIU (Individual Unemployability — paid at the 100% rate); (3) 100% rating via military disability retirement; (4) Gold Star Spouse. NOT a full exemption — only half of the first $200K is exempt; value above $200K remains fully taxable. Apply at county assessor between January 1 and July 1; once approved, no need to reapply unless VA status changes.
value_description: 50% of first $200K of actual value exempt — typical savings $400–$1,400/yr
how_to_claim: Apply at county assessor between Jan 1 and Jul 1 with VA letter showing 100% P&T or TDIU
agency: County Assessor / CO Division of Veterans Affairs

description: Free Centennial Pass for veterans with 60%+ disability.
how_to_claim: Apply at CPW office with VA letter

description: Free license plates for disabled veterans rated 50%+.
how_to_claim: Apply at county motor vehicle office

description: Full tuition waiver at state universities and colleges for 100% P&T veterans.
how_to_claim: Apply through school veterans office

description: Preference in state employment for disabled veterans. 10+ points added to exam scores.
how_to_claim: Indicate veteran status on application

description: Florida Partial Veterans Exemptions — TWO programs. (1) Under FS 196.24, veterans with 10%+ service-connected disability receive an additional $5,000 reduction in homestead assessed value (on top of standard homestead exemption). (2) Under FS 196.082, veterans age 65+ with COMBAT-RELATED service-connected disability receive a percentage discount on homestead taxes equal to their VA disability rating (e.g., 70% combat-related = 70% off the property tax bill). Both require Florida residency and homestead status. Apply at county property appraiser by March 1.
value_description: $5,000 assessed-value reduction; combat-related 65+ get rating-percentage discount on entire bill
how_to_claim: Apply at county property appraiser by March 1 with VA letter; combat-related discount also requires age 65+ and combat-related determination from VA

description: Florida Total Property Tax Exemption (FS 196.081) — full exemption from ALL ad valorem property taxes on the homestead for veterans honorably discharged with a service-connected total and PERMANENT disability. NO cap on home value, NO income test. Veteran must be a permanent Florida resident. Surviving unremarried spouse may continue the exemption for life and may transfer it to a new homestead. Apply at the county property appraiser by March 1 with VA letter certifying 100% P&T.
value_description: Full property tax exemption on homestead — typical savings $2,500–$6,000/yr depending on home value/county
how_to_claim: Apply at county property appraiser by March 1 with VA letter certifying total and permanent service-connected disability
agency: County Property Appraiser / FL Dept of Revenue

benefit_name: Florida State Parks Military Entrance Pass
description: Florida State Parks Military Entrance Pass — FREE LIFETIME pass to all 175 Florida State Parks for honorably discharged veterans with ANY service-connected disability rating (1%+, not just 100%). Pass admits up to 8 people at most locations. Veterans without service-connected disability get 25% off annual pass; FL residents who are 100% disabled OR age 65+ get 50% off camping fees. Pass must be obtained IN PERSON at any state park with VA disability documentation and DD-214.
value_description: Free lifetime entrance pass for any rating — annual value ~$60; admits up to 8 people
how_to_claim: Obtain in person at any FL State Park with VA disability documentation and DD-214 showing honorable discharge
agency: FL Dept of Environmental Protection / FL State Parks

description: Free DV plate and no registration fees for disabled veterans.
how_to_claim: Apply at county tax collector

description: Georgia HERO (Helping Educate Reservists and their Offspring) Scholarship. Despite the existing description, this is NOT a scholarship for dependents of disabled vets generally — it is specifically for: (1) active members of the GA National Guard or US Military Reserves who deployed for 181+ cumulative days in a designated combat zone since Feb 1, 2003 (or less if evacuated due to severe injuries); (2) the spouse of a qualifying member who was killed or disabled in combat; (3) dependent children of qualifying members, born before the deployment, age 25 or younger. Award: up to $2,000 per academic year, max 4 academic years ($8,000 lifetime). Children earn one award year per 181 days of qualifying combat service by the parent. Apply via Georgia Student Finance Commission.
how_to_claim: Apply at gafutures.org via the Georgia Student Finance Commission with proof of combat-zone service

description: Disabled Veteran Homestead Exemption — exempts up to $126,526 (2026 amount, indexed annually) of the homestead's ASSESSED value from all ad valorem property taxes (state, county, municipal, school). Georgia assessed value = 40% of fair market value, so $126,526 of assessed value shields about $316,315 of home value. The portion above that remains taxable — it is NOT a full property tax exemption. Eligible: 100% service-connected disabled veterans, vets compensated at 100% for individual unemployability (TDIU), or vets with specific severe disabilities (loss of use of limbs, blindness, etc.). Unremarried surviving spouse and minor children also qualify. File at the local county tax commissioner with VA disability documentation, typically by April 1 of the tax year.
value_description: Up to $126,526 (2026) deducted from assessed value; typical savings ~$3,000–$4,500/yr depending on millage
how_to_claim: File at local county tax commissioner with VA disability award letter; deadline typically April 1

description: Two separate Georgia veteran hunting/fishing benefits. (1) FREE One-Time Veterans License — full sportsman's license (hunting, big game, migratory bird stamp, salt and freshwater fishing, mountain trout) valid for 1 year. Available to Georgia-resident veterans who served 90+ days active federal duty and were honorably discharged on or after July 1, 2005. NO disability rating required, but can only be issued once per veteran. (2) Disability Sportsman's License — 3-year heavily discounted (NOT free) license for veterans with a permanent and total (100% P&T) disability rating from the VA. Veterans with a mental impairment disability are eligible for a free honorary fishing license only. Both licenses require mailing the application with documentation to GA DNR — cannot be obtained or renewed online.
value_description: One-Time license is free (~$170 value); Disability license is steeply discounted over 3 years
how_to_claim: Mail application + DD-214 (One-Time) or VA P&T letter (Disability) to GA DNR. Call 800-366-2661.

description: Free Disabled Veteran (DV) license plate for Georgia veterans with a 100% service-connected disability rating OR rated individually unemployable and paid at the 100% rate (TDIU). Veterans may receive up to 2 free DV plates for personal-use vehicles (cars or motorcycles). The first vehicle bearing the DV plate is also exempt from ad valorem and TAVT (title ad valorem tax). Purple Heart recipients qualify for a separate (also free) Purple Heart plate with the same tax benefits — Purple Heart eligibility requires only the PH certificate or DD-214 PH designation, NOT a disability rating.
value_description: Plate fees + 1 vehicle worth of ad valorem/TAVT — typically $200-$600/yr
how_to_claim: Apply at county tag office with VA award letter showing 100% rating (or TDIU paid at 100% rate); Purple Heart applicants bring DD-214 or PH certificate
agency: Georgia Department of Revenue / County Tag Office

description: Illinois Veteran Grant (IVG) pays mandatory tuition and certain fees at Illinois public 2-year and 4-year colleges/universities for eligible Illinois veterans. Eligibility: served at least 12 months active federal duty (or shorter for active-duty deaths/medical discharge), entered service from Illinois, returned to Illinois within 6 months of discharge, honorable discharge. NOT contingent on disability rating but the grant is funded by appropriation each year — schools may not be reimbursed in full, and the Veteran is responsible for any difference. Apply via ISAC.
how_to_claim: Apply through Illinois Student Assistance Commission (ISAC) using IVG application

benefit_name: Standard Homestead Exemption for Veterans with Disabilities (SHEVD)
description: Illinois SHEVD reduces the equalized assessed value (EAV) of a disabled veteran's primary residence on a tiered scale: 30%–49% rating = $2,500 EAV reduction; 50%–69% rating = $5,000 EAV reduction; 70%+ rating = up to $250,000 EAV reduction (effectively full exemption for most homes). 100% P&T veterans are auto-renewed each year. Cannot be combined with the general Homestead Exemption for Persons with Disabilities or the Returning Veterans Homeowner Exemption. Apply with the county Chief County Assessment Office.
value_description: $2,500–$250,000 EAV reduction depending on rating; ~$200–$8,000+/yr in tax savings
how_to_claim: File with County Assessor / Chief County Assessment Officer with VA disability letter
agency: Illinois Department of Revenue / County Assessor

description: Illinois disabled veterans with a 10%+ service-connected disability are admitted FREE to Illinois state parks day-use, and disabled veterans (any rating) plus former POWs receive free camping at IDNR campgrounds. Veterans with disabilities also get a free Disabled Veteran Hunting/Fishing license. Apply at IDNR.
value_description: Park admission + camping fees waived (~$200/yr value)
how_to_claim: Show DD-214 or VA disability letter at park entrance / IDNR license vendor

description: Free Illinois disabled veteran (DV) license plates for veterans with 50%+ service-connected disability. The DV plate exempts the veteran from registration fee on one passenger vehicle. Apply through Illinois Secretary of State.
how_to_claim: Apply at IL Secretary of State

benefit_name: Children of Disabled Veterans (CVO) Tuition Exemption
description: Indiana CVO program provides up to 100% of tuition + mandatory fees at Indiana PUBLIC colleges/universities for biological/adopted (NOT step-)children of disabled veterans or Purple Heart recipients. Up to 124 semester credit-hours at the undergraduate resident rate over 8 academic years. Eligibility: veteran-parent listed Indiana as home of record at enlistment OR child resided in Indiana 5+ years before applying; child must apply before age 33. Funded by state appropriation — coverage percentage can be reduced if funds run short. Step-children NOT eligible.
value_description: Up to 100% tuition + fees at IN public schools (124 credit-hours over 8 yrs)
how_to_claim: Apply through ScholarTrack; eligibility determined by Indiana DVA
agency: Indiana Commission for Higher Education / IN DVA

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran Property Tax Credit (HB1001 / 2025 reform)
description: Indiana SIGNIFICANTLY revamped disabled veteran property tax relief in 2025 — converting the prior $24,960/$14,000 deduction into a tax credit on the final bill. As of 2026: veterans rated 100% service-connected disabled (or TDIU) receive a 100% credit on property taxes for their primary residence with NO home value cap (the prior $240,000 cap was removed). Veterans with at least 10% rating who served during wartime continue to qualify for the $24,960 deduction (assessed value cap $240,000 still applies to that program). Both surviving spouses qualify under specific conditions. File with the county auditor.
value_description: 100% credit for 100% disabled (no home value cap); $24,960 deduction for 10%+ wartime
how_to_claim: File at county auditor with DD-214 + current VA disability award letter
agency: Indiana Department of Veterans Affairs / County Auditor

description: Indiana disabled veterans with VA-rated service-connected disability receive a free Golden Hoosier Passport / Annual Park Permit equivalent for state park entry. Apply at any Indiana DNR property.
how_to_claim: Request at state park entrance

description: Free Indiana disabled veteran (DV) license plate for veterans with a service-connected disability (any rating that qualifies for VA-issued disability license per BMV). Free annual registration on one vehicle.
how_to_claim: Apply at BMV

description: Edward T. Conroy Memorial Scholarship for Maryland residents who are: (a) US Armed Forces veterans declared 100% disabled service-connected by the VA, (b) sons/daughters/spouses/surviving spouses of qualifying veterans, (c) POW/MIA dependents, (d) state/federal public safety employees disabled in line of duty. Awards up to the equivalent of full tuition + mandatory fees at MD public 4-year institutions; lower amounts at private/2-year. Must attend MD school. Apply through MHEC.
value_description: Up to full tuition + fees at MD public 4-yr; ~$9,000–$13,000/yr
how_to_claim: Apply through Maryland Higher Education Commission (MHEC)

description: Maryland Disabled Veterans Property Tax Exemption — 100% exemption from real property tax on the dwelling house and surrounding curtilage for veterans rated 100% service-connected PERMANENT AND TOTAL by the VA. Eligibility STRICTLY requires P&T (not just 100% schedular without permanence). Surviving spouse may continue if not remarried. Apply directly to county SDAT office with VA rating decision documenting 100% SC P&T, DD-214, and proof of MD residency. No filing deadline — can apply any time.
value_description: Full real property tax exemption on primary residence; ~$3,000–$8,000/yr
how_to_claim: Submit Application for Exemption for Disabled Veterans to local SDAT office
agency: Maryland State Department of Assessments and Taxation (SDAT)

description: Free Maryland vehicle registration / disabled veteran license plate for veterans with 100% service-connected disability. Title fee and excise tax exemption may also apply. Apply at MVA.
how_to_claim: Apply at MVA

description: Michigan Children of Veterans Tuition Grant — undergraduate tuition assistance up to $2,800/year (NOT full tuition) for natural or adopted children of a Michigan veteran who (a) died on active federal duty, OR (b) has a VA-rated 100% total and permanent service-connected disability. Student must be 16–26 years old, hold a high school diploma/GED, enroll at least half-time at a participating MI institution, and maintain ≥2.25 cumulative GPA. Maximum 4 academic years. Administered by MI Department of Treasury / MI Student Aid.
value_description: Up to $2,800/yr for max 4 years (capped — not full tuition)
how_to_claim: Apply through MI Student Aid; provide DD-214 and VA P&T letter
agency: Michigan Department of Treasury (MI Student Aid)

description: Michigan Disabled Veterans Exemption (MCL 211.7b) — full exemption from real property tax on the homestead. Eligibility (any one of): (1) VA-rated 100% permanent and total service-connected disability; (2) compensated at 100% via Individual Unemployability (TDIU); OR (3) recipient of VA Specially Adapted Housing (SAH) assistance. Surviving unremarried spouse may continue. Apply to local assessor with Form 5107 by December 31. NEW 2026 reform: once approved, the exemption stays in place automatically — no more annual reapplication unless the property/owner changes.
value_description: Full property tax exemption on homestead — typical savings $2,500–$5,000/yr depending on home value and millage
how_to_claim: File Form 5107 with local assessor by December 31; no annual reapply after 2026
agency: Local City/Township Assessor (Michigan Treasury STC oversight)

description: Free DV plates for 100% disabled veterans.
how_to_claim: Apply at Secretary of State office

description: Missouri Wartime Veterans' Survivors Grant — tuition + room/board allowance for surviving spouses and children of MO-resident POST-9/11 combat veterans who EITHER died from combat action OR became 80% disabled from injuries/accidents sustained in combat after 9/11/2001. The veteran parent must have a DD-214 showing combat pay tax exclusion / hostile fire pay / imminent danger pay. NOT available for general 80%+ disabled veterans. Grant covers: tuition (up to MU Columbia in-state rate), $2,000/semester room and board, and up to $500/year for books. Award is competitively limited to 25 students per year (statutory cap).
value_description: Tuition + up to $2K/sem R&B + $500/yr books — limited to 25 awards/yr
how_to_claim: Apply via Missouri State Financial Aid Portal; veteran must have post-9/11 combat DD-214
agency: Missouri Department of Higher Education and Workforce Development

description: Missouri Property Tax Credit (PTC, "circuit breaker") — NOT an exemption. A refundable income-tax credit that reimburses a portion of property tax (or rent) paid. For 100% service-connected disabled veterans: maximum credit is $1,100/year (homeowners who lived in the home all year) or $750/year (renters or part-year owners). Strict income limits: owners must have household income ≤$30,000 single / $34,000 married; renters ≤$27,200 / $29,200. Note: VA disability compensation IS counted as income for this calculation. Filed annually with MO Form MO-PTC. There is NO general property tax EXEMPTION for disabled veterans in Missouri — proposed legislation HB921/HB2672 (2026 session) would create one but had not passed as of April 2026.
value_description: Refundable credit up to $1,100/yr for owners, $750/yr for renters — income-tested
how_to_claim: File MO Form MO-PTC with state income tax return

description: Free license plates for 60%+ disabled veterans.
how_to_claim: Apply at DOR

description: Up to 4 years of scholarship for children of certain disabled veterans.
how_to_claim: Apply through NC Division of Veterans Affairs

description: North Carolina Disabled Veterans Property Tax Exclusion (NCDVA-9). Excludes from property tax appraisal the FIRST $100,000 of the appraised value of the homestead (current 2025-2026 tax year). Effective July 1, 2026 the exclusion amount RISES to the first $150,000 of appraised value. Eligibility: honorably discharged veteran with (1) 100% permanent and total service-connected disability OR (2) receipt of VA Specially Adapted Housing benefits under 38 USC 2101. Unmarried surviving spouse of a qualifying veteran also qualifies. NO income test. The portion of value above the exclusion remains taxable. File NCDVA-9 with the county tax office.
value_description: $100,000 of appraised value excluded (rises to $150,000 on 2026-07-01); typical savings ~$700–$1,500/yr
how_to_claim: File Form NCDVA-9 with your county tax assessor with VA P&T disability documentation

description: North Carolina Disabled Veteran Lifetime Hunting and Fishing License (NC Wildlife Resources Commission). FREE lifetime combination hunting + fishing license for NC-resident veterans with a service-connected disability rating of 50% or higher. Valid for the veteran's lifetime as long as the 50%+ rating is maintained. For veterans with under 50% (but still service-connected), a Disabled Veteran license is available for $14. NCDMVA must certify eligibility before NC Wildlife issues the license.
value_description: Free lifetime hunting+fishing license for 50%+ disabled vets (~$600 value over 30 years vs paying $20/yr)
how_to_claim: Submit Hunting and Fishing License Application via NC DMVA for certification, then NC Wildlife issues the license
agency: NC DMVA / NC Wildlife Resources Commission

description: Free DV plates for disabled veterans. 100% disabled get free registration.
how_to_claim: Apply at DMV

description: Full tuition at SUNY/CUNY for eligible veterans.
how_to_claim: Apply through HESC

description: New York Alternative Veterans Exemption (RPTL §458-a). Reduces assessed value of primary residence: 15% for wartime service, +10% for combat-zone service, +50% × VA disability rating for service-connected disability. Caps vary by locality. Applies to county/city/town/village/school taxes (school portion only where district has opted in), NOT special districts or special assessments. Important 2026 change: a Feb 2026 amendment makes a FULL property tax exemption mandatory STATEWIDE for veterans with a 100% permanent service-connected disability — effective Oct 1, 2026, applicable to the 2027 assessment roll. Until then the 100% exemption is still locally opted in. Application deadline is March 1 in most communities; file with the local assessor.
value_description: 15%+10%+50%×rating off assessed value (varies by locality); 100% disabled get full exemption from the 2027 roll forward
how_to_claim: File RP-458-a with local assessor by March 1; for 100% disabled, file RP-458-b after the new mandate takes effect

description: Free Empire Pass for disabled veterans.
how_to_claim: Apply at state park

description: Free registration for 100% disabled veterans.
how_to_claim: Apply at DMV

description: Tuition aid for children of disabled/deceased veterans at Ohio schools.
how_to_claim: Apply through OH Dept of Higher Education

description: Ohio Disabled Veteran Homestead Exemption. Reduces taxable market value of the homestead by $50,000 (vs. the standard senior homestead of about $28,000). Eligibility: any veteran with a 100% total service-connected disability rating (or paid at 100% via TDIU). NO income test for the disabled veteran tier — unlike the regular senior homestead which is income-tested. Surviving spouses of qualifying first responders get an enhanced $58,000 reduction. The portion of market value above $50,000 remains taxable. File Form DTE 105I with the county auditor.
value_description: $50,000 of market value exempt; typical savings ~$700–$1,400/yr depending on county effective millage
how_to_claim: File Form DTE 105I with your county auditor by Dec 31 of the tax year with VA disability documentation
agency: County Auditor / Ohio Dept of Taxation

description: Free camping for 100% disabled veterans at state parks.
how_to_claim: Show VA ID at campground

description: Free DV plates for disabled veterans rated 100%.
how_to_claim: Apply at BMV

description: Two PA programs. (1) Educational Gratuity Program (EGP): for the CHILDREN of honorably discharged wartime veterans who have a service-connected total and permanent disability, OR children of veterans who died in service during a period of war or armed conflict. Award: up to $500 per term for up to 8 terms ($4,000 lifetime, NOT per year). Child must be age 16-23, have lived in PA for 5 years prior to applying, attend a PA school, and demonstrate financial need. NOT for the disabled veteran themselves. (2) Postsecondary Educational Gratuity Program (PEGP): full tuition + fees + on-campus room and board waiver at PA public postsecondary institutions for the CHILDREN of PA National Guard members and other public-safety/military personnel killed in the line of duty after January 1, 1976 (or after 9/11/2001 for federal/state active military duty deaths).
value_description: EGP $500/term × 8 terms = $4,000 lifetime; PEGP covers full tuition + fees + room/board (much higher value, narrower eligibility)
how_to_claim: EGP: contact your county Director of Veterans Affairs. PEGP: 800-692-7392 or pegp@pheaa.org

description: Pennsylvania Disabled Veterans Real Estate Tax Exemption — full exemption from county, municipal, and school real estate taxes on the veteran's primary residence (and up to 5 acres). NOT automatic for any 100% rating. Eligibility requires ALL of: (1) 100% schedular service-connected disability, OR TDIU, OR service-connected blindness/paraplegia/loss of 2+ limbs; (2) wartime service (period of war per VA, or qualifying expeditionary medal); (3) financial need — presumed if gross annual income is at or below $114,637 (2026 threshold, indexed); above that you must show monthly expenses exceed monthly income. Surviving unremarried spouse may continue the exemption. Apply through the county Veterans Affairs Director with the State Veterans Commission making the final determination.
value_description: Full real estate tax exemption on primary residence — averages ~$3,000–$5,000/yr depending on county/school millage. Income test applies.
how_to_claim: Apply through county Director of Veterans Affairs; final determination by PA State Veterans Commission. Income documentation required.
agency: PA Dept of Military and Veterans Affairs / County VA Director

description: PA state parks have no entry fee for any visitor — admission is universally free, this is NOT a veterans-specific benefit. There is no PA state-park vets pass equivalent to other states. Adjacent benefits: (1) the new (2026) DCNR Conservation Volunteer program awards free camping/cabin nights for volunteer hours (not veteran-specific); (2) seniors 62+ get camping discounts; (3) federal America the Beautiful Lifetime Pass for permanently disabled veterans (any rating) provides free entry to NATIONAL parks, not state. Listed here for completeness so users do not look for a PA-specific veteran perk that does not exist.
value_description: No PA-specific veteran benefit — parks are free for everyone
how_to_claim: No application — PA state parks are open to all without entry fee
agency: PA Department of Conservation and Natural Resources

description: Two PA programs for veterans. (1) FREE Disabled Veteran Resident Hunting/Furtaker License (PA Game Commission) and FREE Resident Fishing License (PA Fish & Boat Commission) for veterans with 100% service-connected disability rating. (2) Reduced-fee hunting and fishing licenses (~$2 each) available for veterans with less than 100% disability. Both require PA residency. Apply via county treasurer or directly through PGC/PFBC with proof of disability.
value_description: Free for 100% disabled; ~$2 reduced fee for partial ratings
how_to_claim: Apply at county treasurer or PA Game Commission / PA Fish & Boat Commission with VA disability award letter
agency: PA Game Commission / PA Fish & Boat Commission

description: Pennsylvania eliminated all vehicle registration and title fees for 100% Permanent and Total (P&T) service-connected disabled veterans starting in 2023 — this continues through 2026. The Disabled Veteran (DV / IW) license plate itself has a separate $11 plate fee (one-time). Eligibility requires a VA Summary of Benefits Letter showing 100% service-connected disability. Apply at any PennDOT driver license center or by mail using Form MV-145V. Processing 2-4 weeks.
value_description: Eliminates ~$45/yr registration + title fees in perpetuity; one-time $11 plate fee remains
how_to_claim: Submit PennDOT Form MV-145V with VA Summary of Benefits Letter showing 100% SC disability

description: South Carolina Free Tuition for Children of Certain War Veterans (SC Code §59-111-50). Tuition (NOT fees) waived at any SC state-supported college/university for CHILDREN age 26 or under of veterans who: were killed in action, died from service-connected causes, are 100% permanent and total service-connected disabled, are POW/MIA, or are Medal of Honor / Purple Heart recipients. Both the student and the qualifying veteran must have been actual SC residents for 12+ continuous months prior to application and remain residents. NOT for the veteran themselves — this is a dependent benefit. Fees, books, and room/board remain the family's responsibility.
value_description: Tuition (not fees) waived at SC state colleges for children up to age 26
how_to_claim: Apply via SC Department of Veterans Affairs at scdva.sc.gov/education

description: South Carolina Disabled Veterans Property Tax Exemption (SC Code §12-37-220). FULL exemption from state and local property taxes on the dwelling house and the lot it sits on, owned and used by a veteran with 100% permanent and total service-connected disability — may be jointly owned with a spouse. Same exemption extends to a person who is paraplegic or hemiplegic, or who has Parkinson's, MS, or ALS that causes equivalent ambulatory difficulty. Surviving unremarried spouse continues the exemption. Separate motor vehicle exemption: up to TWO private passenger vehicles owned/leased by the disabled veteran are also exempt from property tax (and one for surviving spouse).
value_description: FULL home property tax exemption + 2 vehicles exempt from property tax — typical home savings ~$2,000–$5,000/yr
how_to_claim: File application (PT-401-I) with SC Department of Revenue with VA P&T disability award letter; SCDOR processes all home exemptions

description: Free hunting and fishing licenses for 100% disabled veterans.
how_to_claim: Apply at SCDNR

description: Free DV plates for disabled veterans.
how_to_claim: Apply at DMV

description: Tennessee has no state income tax.
how_to_claim: No action needed

description: Tennessee Disabled Veterans Property Tax Relief. NOT an exemption — the state REIMBURSES county trustees for the property tax paid by qualifying disabled veterans on the first $175,000 of the home's market value. NO income limit (unlike the senior/disabled-homeowner relief tier). Eligibility: honorable (or other-than-dishonorable) discharge AND a VA-rated 100% permanent and total service-connected disability OR paraplegia/paid at 100% TDIU/loss of two or more limbs/legal blindness from service. Year 1 you pay the bill in full and submit proof to receive a state refund; in subsequent years you receive a voucher that pre-reduces the bill. Surviving unremarried spouse continues the relief. Apply at the County Trustee.
value_description: Tax relief on first $175,000 market value — typical reimbursement ~$1,500–$3,500/yr depending on county tax rate
how_to_claim: Apply at your County Trustee with VA P&T disability award letter; year 1 pay first then submit proof for state refund

description: Free DV plates for disabled veterans.
how_to_claim: Apply at county clerk

description: Texas Hazlewood Act — exempts qualified Texas veterans from tuition and most fees at Texas public colleges/universities for up to 150 credit hours. Eligibility: entered service from Texas (Texas residency at enlistment), 181+ days active duty, honorable discharge, ineligible for federal GI Bill education benefits OR has exhausted them. The Hazlewood Legacy Act allows transfer of UNUSED hours to one dependent child (under 26) of a Texas veteran — but only if the veteran is currently a Texas resident. Federally administered "Hazlewood for Spouses" applies to surviving spouses of KIA / 100% P&T / MIA / POW veterans.
value_description: Up to ~$15,000–$30,000 in tuition + most mandatory fees waived (excludes books and room/board)
how_to_claim: Apply through the school's veterans/financial aid office with DD-214 and Hazlewood form; verification through Texas Veterans Commission.

description: Preference in state government hiring for disabled veterans.
how_to_claim: Indicate veteran status on state job applications. Provide DD-214 and VA disability letter.

description: Texas 100% Disabled Veteran Homestead Exemption — total exemption from all ad valorem property taxes on the veteran's residence homestead, with NO cap on home value. Eligible: veterans rated 100% service-connected by the VA, OR paid at the 100% rate due to Individual Unemployability (IU) — Texas treats IU the same as 100% schedular. Unremarried surviving spouse keeps the full exemption for life on the same homestead. Applies only to the homestead (one residence, plus up to 20 acres if used for residential purposes). File Form 50-114 with the county appraisal district (NOT the tax office); deadline April 30, with up to 5 years of retroactive filing allowed.
value_description: Full property tax exemption — typical savings $3,000–$8,000+/yr depending on home value and county/school rates
how_to_claim: File Form 50-114 with county appraisal district. Provide VA award letter showing 100% rating or 100% IU.

description: Texas Partial Disabled Veteran Homestead Exemption — flat dollar amount deducted from the homestead's assessed value, scaled by VA disability rating: 10–29% rating = $5,000 off; 30–49% = $7,500 off; 50–69% = $10,000 off; 70–99% = $12,000 off. NOT a full exemption — applies only to a flat dollar amount of assessed value. Eligible vets at 100% / IU should use the Total Property Tax Exemption instead (id=1). Veterans 65+ with at least 10% disability automatically receive the $12,000 amount regardless of rating. File Form 50-114 with the county appraisal district.
value_description: $5,000 to $12,000 off assessed value — typical savings $100–$400/yr depending on local millage
how_to_claim: File Form 50-114 with county appraisal district with VA disability rating letter.

description: Veterans with 60%+ disability receive a free Texas State Parks Pass.
how_to_claim: Apply online at Texas Parks & Wildlife or at any state park entrance. Bring VA disability letter.

description: Free specialized license plates for veterans with 50%+ disability. Plates exempt one vehicle from registration fees.
how_to_claim: Apply at your county tax office with VA disability letter and vehicle title.

description: One vehicle registration fee waived for veterans with 50%+ disability who have DV plates.
how_to_claim: Automatic when using DV plates at county tax office.

description: Full tuition/fees at VA state schools for dependents of disabled/deceased veterans.
how_to_claim: Apply through SCHEV
agency: State Council of Higher Education for Virginia

description: Virginia Disabled Veteran Real Property Tax Exemption — full exemption from real property taxes on the veteran's principal residence (the dwelling and up to 1 acre). Eligibility: VA-rated 100% service-connected, permanent AND total disability, OR rated less than 100% but paid at the 100% rate due to Individual Unemployability AND rated permanent and total. Unremarried surviving spouse may continue the exemption if the veteran died on or after January 1, 2011. Apply through the local county/city Commissioner of the Revenue with a VA Summary of Benefits letter.
value_description: Full real property tax exemption on primary residence — typical savings $3,000–$8,000/yr depending on locality
how_to_claim: Apply at local Commissioner of the Revenue with VA Summary of Benefits letter showing 100% P&T (or TDIU rated P&T).

description: Free admission to all Virginia state parks for disabled veterans.
how_to_claim: Show VA ID at entrance

description: Free vehicle registration and DV plates for disabled veterans.
how_to_claim: Apply at DMV

description: Tuition waiver at state schools for eligible veterans and dependents.
how_to_claim: Apply at school veterans office

description: Washington has no state income tax.
how_to_claim: No action needed

description: Washington Property Tax Exemption for Veterans with Disabilities — a TIERED REDUCTION (not a full exemption) on property taxes for the primary residence, based on income. Eligibility: VA service-connected rating of 80%+ OR receiving compensation at the 100% rate. Income limit applies (combined disposable household income), set by county — typically ~$40,000 (rural counties) up to ~$89,000 (King County) for 2025–2026; thresholds re-set every 3 years. CRITICAL: VA disability compensation and DIC are NOT counted toward the income test. The reduction tier (Threshold 1, 2, or 3) determines whether you get a frozen value, partial exemption, or fuller relief. Apply at the county assessor annually.
value_description: Tiered reduction (frozen value to substantial exemption) — typical savings $1,500–$4,500/yr depending on income tier
how_to_claim: Apply at county assessor with VA disability letter and income documentation; deadline varies by county (typically Jan–May).
agency: County Assessor (Washington Department of Revenue oversight)

description: Free DV plates for disabled veterans.
how_to_claim: Apply at DOL
